05 May 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, May 5, 2026 SOUTHCOM 05 May 2026 -- On May 5, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.

  • Coast Guard, U.S. Navy, interdicts suspected drug vessel off Haiti US Coast Guard 05 May 2026 -- A Coast Guard law enforcement detachment deployed on the USS Billings stopped a suspected drug smuggling vessel, Thursday, approximately 8 miles off Mole Saint-Nicolas, Haiti.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Transcript: Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine Hold a Press Briefing DoD 05 May 2026 -- SECRETARY OF WAR PETE HEGSETH: Well, as you know, President Trump has directed U.S. Central Command to restart the free flow of commerce through the Strait of Hormuz under the umbrella of Project Freedom. To be clear, this operation is separate and distinct from Operation Epic Fury. Project Freedom is defensive in nature, focused in scope and temporary in duration, with one mission: protecting innocent commercial shipping from Iranian aggression.
  • 'Project Freedom' Aims to Get Thousands of Commercial Ships Safely Through Strait of Hormuz DoD News 05 May 2026 -- Iran has blocked safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz for commercial cargo and tanker vessels, trapping thousands inside the Persian Gulf and halting the free flow of commerce in the region.
  • Hegseth Says Cease-Fire Holding, After Iran Launches Missiles, US Sinks Boats RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- The US cease-fire with Iran remains in place, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, a day after Iran launched missiles at the United Arab Emirates, and the US military sank six Iranian small boats.
  • Hegseth Says US-Iran Ceasefire Continues to Hold, Calls Project Freedom 'Distinct' Sputnik 05 May 2026 -- The ceasefire between the United States and Iran is holding, US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said on Tuesday, adding that the so called Project Freedom initiative is a "distinct project".
  • US attacks civilian cargo boats heading to Iran's shores, killing 5 on board: Report Press TV 05 May 2026 -- The United States has attacked two civilian cargo boats heading from the Omani coast to the Iranian shoreline, claiming the lives of at least five people on board, a report says.

  • UAE Says Attacked Again By Iranian Missiles And Drones RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates has said that it has once again been attacked by Iranian missiles and drones and was responding to these threats.
  • Iran Denies Attacks On UAE, Warns Against Retaliation RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- Iran has denied that it carried out any attacks on the United Arab Emirates, while warning that it would deliver a "crushing and regret-inducing response" to any attack launched from UAE territory.
  • Iran Again Warn Vessels To Use Its Designated Hormuz Corridor RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- The naval command of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has again warned all vessels intending to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, saying the only safe route is the corridor previously designated by the Islamic republic.
  • May 5: Iran activates new Strait of Hormuz transit system as US blockade ends in failure Press TV 05 May 2026 -- Iran tightened its control over the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday, rolling out a formal mechanism for regulating all maritime traffic through the world's most sensitive oil chokepoint, just hours after the United States killed at least five civilians by attacking two unarmed cargo boats traveling from Oman to the Iranian coast.
  • IRGC warns ships must exclusively use designated routes in Hormuz Strait Press TV 05 May 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned ships seeking to pass the Strait of Hormuz to exclusively use routes designated by Iran as safe.
  • Exclusive: Iran launches new maritime mechanism for vessels transiting Strait of Hormuz Press TV 05 May 2026 -- Iran has officially launched a new mechanism for governing maritime traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz, Press TV has learned.

  • Over 22,000 People on More Than 1,550 Commercial Ships Trapped in Gulf - Pentagon Sputnik 05 May 2026 -- US Joint Chiefs of Staff Chairman Dan Caine said on Tuesday that 22,500 mariners at over 1,500 commercial vessels are currently unable to leave the Gulf area.

  • Trump Says Iran Should 'Wave White Flag,' Urges Deal RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has dismissed Iran's military capabilities and urged Tehran to "do the smart thing" and strike a deal to end the war amid a fragile cease-fire.
  • Trump Questions Value of Iran's Uranium — But Still Demands It Sputnik 05 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump suggested that Iran's enriched uranium "probably can't be used," while simultaneously insisting Washington wants it handed over.
  • The United States Proposes a UN Security Council Resolution to Defend Freedom of Navigation and Secure the Strait of Hormuz US Dept. of State 05 May 2026 -- The Islamic Republic of Iran continues to hold the world's economy hostage with its efforts to close the Strait of Hormuz, threats to attack ships in the Strait, laying of sea mines that pose a danger to shipping, and attempts to charge tolls for the world's most important waterway.
  • Trump Warns Iran Will Be 'Blown Off Face Of The Earth' If US Vessels Targeted In Hormuz RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has threatened that Iran would be "blown off the face of the earth" if it targets US vessels seeking to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, as tensions strain a fragile cease-fire between the two sides.

  • Iran warns UAE: Refrain from aligning with aggressors Press TV 05 May 2026 -- The Foreign Ministry has called on the United Arab Emirates to discontinue its alignment and cooperation with the parties that are hostile towards Iran, saying the Islamic Republic reserves all rights to respond in kind.
  • Iran will never submit to coercion; threats, bullying to lead nowhere: President Press TV 05 May 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian strongly discourages the United States against continuing to resort to its overbearing methods to try to exact concessions from Iran.
  • Jebel Ali collapse transforms Iran's logistical position Press TV 05 May 2026 -- The halt of activity at Jebel Ali port in the United Arab Emirates due to heightened tensions in the Persian Gulf has forced Iran to accelerate a long-planned diversification of its trade routes.
  • FM Araghchi to travel to Beijing for continued diplomatic consultations with key partners Press TV 05 May 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi will travel to Beijing on Tuesday for high-level talks with his Chinese counterpart on bilateral ties and regional developments.
  • Qalibaf: Strait of Hormuz situation 'unbearable' for US while Tehran has 'not even started yet' Press TV 05 May 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf has warned that the ongoing situation in the Strait of Hormuz is becoming "unbearable" for the United States, while Tehran has "not even started yet."
  • IRGC deputy chief: US will suffer severe repercussions if it escalates tensions against Iran Press TV 05 May 2026 -- A high-ranking commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has warned that the repercussions of any further escalation against Iran will be considerably severe for the United States, stressing that Washington will utilize all its military power but will be defeated at the hands of Iranian forces.
  • Iran rejects Arab League resolution: Shielding perpetuators will not absolve intl. obligations Press TV 05 May 2026 -- Iran has categorically rejected a recent resolution adopted by the Arab League, saying "no attempt to shield the perpetrators of aggression" will absolve those responsible of their international obligations.
  • Iran Warns UAE Against 'Military Solution' As Tensions Escalate RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- In a new message, Iran's foreign minister has urged the United Arab Emirates not to resort to a "military solution" against the Islamic republic.

  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova regarding developments concerning Iran Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 May 2026

  • Iran: Statement by the Spokesperson on latest attacks against neighbouring countries European External Action Service (EEAS) 05 May 2026 -- The EU strongly condemns Iran's unprovoked missiles and drone strikes against our strategic partners in the Gulf.
  • United Arab Emirates - France condemns the latest Iranian strikes France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 05 May 2026
  • South Korea Weighs Role In US Hormuz Mission After Ship Blast RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- South Korea has said it would "review its position" on joining US-led operations in the Strait of Hormuz after Donald Trump blamed Iran for an apparent attack on a South Korean-operated vessel.
  • PM statement on strikes targeting UAE: 4 May 2026 Gov.UK 05 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Keir Starmer's statement on strikes targeting UAE.

  • Robert Murrett: A US Vice-Admiral On The 'Very Tough Challenge' Of Hormuz RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- As tensions surge in the Strait of Hormuz following President Donald Trump's announcement of Project Freedom, an effort to get commercial shipping traffic flowing, the fragile cease-fire between Washington and Tehran appears increasingly strained.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • 82nd Airborne Division Ready to Respond, Support Project Freedom, Caine Says DoD News 05 May 2026 -- When the president or the secretary of war needs immediate, scalable and lethal combat power anywhere in the world, 82nd Airborne Division paratroopers answer the call, Air Force Gen. Dan Caine, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, said today during a Pentagon press briefing.
  • Coast Guard Great Lakes District Set to Use Autonomous Sail Drones DoD News 05 May 2026 -- Leveraging a contract awarded by the Coast Guard to enhance maritime domain awareness, the Great Lakes District will deploy autonomous drones to support Coast Guard missions on the Great Lakes from May to October.
  • Army and Defense Sector Announce, 'Right to Integrate' Hackathon Sprint for Shared Technology US Army 05 May 2026 -- Secretary of the Army Dan Driscoll and leaders from Anduril, Boeing, General Dynamics, L3Harris, Leidos, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Palantir, Perennial Autonomy, and RTX announced today that they will "sprint" to conduct a series of combined Army and defense industry partnered hackathon events to integrate critical military technology systems.
  • DEGRE wargame an "Essential Tool" for modern deterrence USSTRATCOM 05 May 2026 -- U.S. Strategic Command, in collaboration with the U.S. Naval War College, conducted the annual Deterrence and Escalation Game and Review to examine and refine strategic deterrence concepts. The exercise brings together military leadership, strategists, and allied partners to explore decision-making in a simulated global crisis.
  • 137th SOW hosts tactical communicators in AFSOC course US Air Force 05 May 2026 -- Expeditionary Communications (XCOMM) Air Commandos from across Air Force Special Operations Command attended the latest iteration of the AFSOC tactical communicator course hosted by 137th Special Operations Wing at Will Rogers Air National Guard Base, Oklahoma City, March 2 - April 17.
  • USS Wichita Returns to Naval Station Mayport US Navy 05 May 2026 -- The Freedom-variant littoral combat ship USS Wichita (LCS 13), operating under U.S. Northern Command (USNORTHCOM) in the Gulf of America, returned to Naval Station Mayport after completing a six-month deployment on May 4, 2026.

  • NATO Begins Exercise STEADFAST DETERRENCE 2026: Securing the High North, Reinforcing Transatlantic Collaboration on Collective Defence NATO - Joint Warfare Centre (JWC) 05 May 2026 -- Exercise STEADFAST DETERRENCE 2026, NATO's premier deterrence and collective defence exercise at the strategic and operational levels, is underway in various locations across Europe and the United States.
  • NATO Deputy Secretary General visits Athens to highlight the importance of a strong defence industry NATO 05 May 2026 -- From 4-5 May 2026, NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska visited Athens to highlight Greece's valuable contributions to the Alliance. Delivering a keynote speech at Defence Exhibition Athens (DEFEA), Ms Shekerinska emphasised the role of industry in keeping NATO's citizens safe.

  • Russia Convinced Nuclear War Must Never Be Unleashed - Foreign Ministry Sputnik 05 May 2026 -- Russia is convinced that a nuclear war must never be unleashed, Russian Foreign Ministry Ambassador-at-Large Andrey Belousov, the head of the Russian delegation to the 2026 Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) Review Conference, said on Tuesday.
  • Rostec Unveils New Swarm Busting System to Crush FPV Drones Sputnik 05 May 2026 -- Rostec, Russia's state technology corporation, announced Tuesday that it has developed a new system to counter mass FPV drone attacks.
  • Meeting with KAMAZ Director General Sergei Kogogin President of Russia 05 May 2026 -- The company's head informed the President about import substitution projects at production facilities, new vehicles, including the K5 series and the line of military products. Social support for the plant's staff and the humanitarian mission in the special military operation zone were in the focus of the discussion.

  • Ukraine - Joint Direct Attack Munitions - Extended Range US Dept. of State 05 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Ukraine for Joint Direct Attack Munitions - Extended Range and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $373.6 million.
  • Ukraine Needs a Unified Coordination Center for the Recruitment of Foreign Nationals into Military Service - Kyrylo Budanov President of Ukraine 05 May 2026

  • New choppers give battalion's training a lift Australia DOD 05 May 2026 -- UH-60M Black Hawk from the 16th Aviation Brigade flew into Townsville to conduct training with soldiers from the 1st Battalion, The Royal Australian Regiment (1RAR).

  • Canada - C-17 Sustainment US Dept. of State 05 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Canada for its purchase of C-17 sustainment services and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $540 million.

  • INDIAN OCEAN SHIP SAGAR ARRIVES AT YANGON, MYANMAR India PIB 05 May 2026 -- The Indian Ocean Ship (IOS) Sagar, INS Sunayna, entered Yangon Harbour on 04 May 2026 as part of India's ongoing maritime engagement in the region.
  • MoD inks Rs 1,476 crore contract with BEL for five Ground-Based Mobile Electronic Systems for Indian Army India PIB 05 May 2026 -- Ministry of Defence has signed a contract with Bharat Electronics Limited, Hyderabad for the procurement of five Ground-Based Mobile Electronic Systems, worth Rs 1,476 crore, for the Indian Army with minimum 72% indigenous content.
  • India's military might forged in factories of our defence industries, Op Sindoor reflected power of indigenous weapons, says Raksha Rajya Mantri at North Tech Symposium 2026 India PIB 05 May 2026 -- "India's military might is forged in the factories of our defence industries," said Raksha Rajya Mantri Shri Sanjay Seth as he credited the success of Operation Sindoor to the unmatched courage and determination of the defence forces, further bolstered by the effectiveness of indigenously-developed state-of-the-art weapons & systems.

  • China calls for high vigilance against Japan's negative moves on nuclear armament issues at UN meeting Global Times 05 May 2026 -- At a review conference of the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) in New York on Monday, Shen Jian, Ambassador for Disarmament Affairs of China, outlined China's position on preventing nuclear proliferation, stating that certain countries are pursuing double standards on non-proliferation, thereby undermining the authority of the treaty and the safeguards and supervision system of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), reported China Central Television (CCTV).
  • China flags Japan's nuclear development at UN NPT review, urges vigilance Global Times 05 May 2026 -- Stepping up its diplomatic efforts, China has once again sounded the alarm at the United Nations over Japan's potential nuclear ambitions, urging the global community to remain highly vigilant.
  • Japan-Philippines Defense Ministers' Joint Press Statement Ministry of Defense of Japan 05 May 2026 -- On May 5, 2026, Minister of Defense of Japan Koizumi Shinjiro and Secretary of National Defense of the Republic of the Philippines Gilberto C. Teodoro, Jr. held a Defense Ministers' Meeting in Manila.
  • Statement by the Defense Ministers of Japan and the Philippines on the Further Promotion of Defense Equipment and Technology Cooperation Ministry of Defense of Japan 05 May 2026

  • Eight years of PESCO: from initiative-building to delivery Permanent Structured Cooperation (PESCO) 05 May 2026

  • OCCAR Highlights European Defence Cooperation at IPC Field Trip in Brussels OCCAR 05 May 2026 -- The first International Procurement Cooperation (IPC) Field Trip brought together representatives from European institutions, national authorities, defence agencies, and industry.

  • European Peace Facility: Council adopts assistance measure under the EPF in support of Senegal EC/CEU 05 May 2026 -- Today, the Council adopted an assistance measure under the European Peace Facility (EPF) in support of the Armed Forces of Senegal in the framework of the Yaoundé Architecture.

  • Suren Papikyan met with Slovak Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence Robert Kaliňák Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 05 May 2026 -- On May 5, the Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia Suren Papikyan met with Slovak Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defence Robert Kaliňák in Warsaw.
  • Defense Ministry: Preparation process for "EFES - 2026" exercise is ongoing Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 05 May 2026 -- Preparation training for the "EFES - 2026" multinational exercise held in the Republic of Türkiye with the participation of Azerbaijani servicemen continues, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • Philippines - Bell 505 Jet Ranger X Helicopter US Dept. of State 05 May 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the Philippines of Bell 505 Jet Ranger X Helicopters and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $150 million.
  • Koizumi cites 'productive' talks as Japan, PH deepen defense ties Philippine News Agency 05 May 2026 -- Japanese Defense Minister Shinjiro Koizumi and Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. on Tuesday reaffirmed their commitment to deepen defense cooperation between Japan and the Philippines, citing an increasingly complex regional security environment.
  • PAF takes part in 'Balikatan' MEDEVAC drills Philippine News Agency 05 May 2026 -- Rescue aircraft and personnel of the Philippine Air Force (PAF) have taken part in the "mass casualty medical evacuation" (MEDEVAC) simulation of this year's "Balikatan."

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Remarks to Press US Dept. of State 05 May 2026 -- "The operation is over. Epic Fury is - the President notified Congress we're done with that stage of it, okay? We're now on to this Project Freedom. As far as a negotiation is concerned, I think the President's been clear that part of the negotiation process has to be not just the enrichment, but what happens to this material that's buried deep somewhere that they have - still have access to if they ever wanted to dig it out. That has to be addressed. And that's being addressed in the negotiation."

    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 5 May 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 05 May 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Israel targets southern Lebanon with internationally banned phosphorus shells Press TV 05 May 2026 -- The Israeli regime's forces are using internationally banned white phosphorus shells against civilian areas in southern Lebanon.
  • Israeli minister Smotrich says regime's borders to expand once wars end Press TV 05 May 2026 -- Israeli minister of finance Bezalel Smotrich says the occupying regime's borders will expand once its wars on several fronts in the West Asia region come to an end, exposing Tel Aviv's expansionist and aggressive intentions under the so-called "Greater Israel" scheme.

  • UNHCR: Despite announced ceasefire, more people are forced to flee their homes in Lebanon UNHCR 05 May 2026 -- Despite an announced ceasefire in place since 17 April, the displacement and humanitarian crisis in Lebanon is far from over. This is a deeply fragile moment, marked by ongoing Israeli airstrikes, shelling, demolitions, evacuation orders, bans on return to certain areas and movement restrictions that continue to drive repeated displacement and rapidly growing humanitarian needs.
  • In Lebanon, the same fears and dangers persist despite ceasefire: UNHCR UN News 05 May 2026 -- Death and destruction have continued unabated in Lebanon while communities are still unable to return to their homes despite a ceasefire that began on 17 April, humanitarians said on Tuesday.

  • The OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians Issues its 127th Report Highlighting the Suffering in the Gaza Strip Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 05 May 2026 -- At least 22 Palestinians were killed and 85 wounded by Israeli forces between 28 April and 4 May 2026, according to the 127th weekly report of the OIC Media Observatory on Israeli Crimes Against Palestinians

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Democratic Republic of the Congo UNOCHA 05 May 2026 -- Relief operations ongoing but needs rise as conflict strains services

  • Healthcare workers must never be targets in conflict: UK statement at the UN Arria meeting on protecting medical care in conflict amid evolving threats UK FCDO 05 May 2026 -- Statement by Jess Jambert-Gray, Deputy Political Coordinator, at the UN Arria meeting on protecting medical care in conflict amid evolving threats.

  • Taiwan minister's visit to Spratlys: a page out of China's 'gray zone' playbook Radio Free Asia 05 May 2026 -- A rare visit by Taiwan's coast guard minister to Taiping Island is part of Taipei's strategy to assert its claim over the disputed South China Sea outpost and a move taken right out of Beijing's "gray zone" playbook, analysts told Radio Free Asia.

  • UN warns of worsening human rights crisis in Mali after deadly attacks UN News 05 May 2026 -- The human rights situation in Mali is rapidly deteriorating following coordinated attacks by armed groups across the country, with civilians killed, displaced and cut off from food and aid, UN rights office OHCHR said on Tuesday.
  • Mali: Civilians impacted amid clashes OHCHR 05 May 2026 -- undertaken in full compliance with international human rights law and norms. Arrest and detention of suspects must be in line with rule of due process, and without discrimination.

  • Peace talks with Reds not in Filipinos' interest - Oban Philippine News Agency 05 May 2026 -- National Security Adviser (NSA) Eduardo Oban on Tuesday threw his support behind Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr.'s rejection of any peace talks with the Communist Party of the Philippines-New People's Army-National Democratic Front (CPP-NPA-NDF).

  • Ministry of Foreign Affairs: Saudi Arabia Strongly Condemns and Denounces Targeting of Site on Khartoum Airport Grounds SPA 05 May 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong condemnation and denunciation of the targeting of a site on the grounds of Khartoum Airport

News Reports

  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov US Dept. of State 05 May 2026
  • UK cracks down on Russia's exploitation of vulnerable migrants and deadly drone capability UK FCDO 05 May 2026 -- The UK has announced new sanctions to curb production of Russian drones and the nefarious networks that are exploiting vulnerable migrants from across the globe to support Russia's illegal war in Ukraine.

  • Xi's spring diplomacy injects stability into volatile world PRC MND 05 May 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping has been busy this spring, leading efforts to drive international cooperation, share development opportunities and uphold fairness and justice, thereby injecting much-needed stability and certainty into a volatile world.

  • State visit not 'breakthrough' but 'basic right': Lai CNA 05 May 2026 -- Bilateral visits between heads of state are a "basic right" and should not be seen as "breakthroughs," President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) said Tuesday upon returning from a delayed trip to Eswatini.
  • Taiwanese have right to engage with the world: Lai CNA 05 May 2026 -- Taiwanese people have the right to engage with the world, said President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Tuesday after making a trip to Eswatini that China allegedly had previously blocked.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 05 May 2026

  • Efforts to Minimize Drought Damage KCNA 05 May 2026 -- According to Rodong Sinmun, scientific farming promotion teams active in different parts of the DPRK strive to minimize the negative impact of the persistent drought on wheat and barley farming.

  • Iran's Nuclear Breakout Time Unchanged After Recent War, Reuters Reports RFE/RL 05 May 2026 -- US intelligence assessments indicate that Iran's nuclear breakout time has not changed since last summer, according to Reuters.

  • Iraq encouraging oil exports through Strait of Hormuz with big discounts Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 05 May 2026 -- Iraq has sharply cut oil prices for buyers willing to ship crude through the Strait of Hormuz amid the escalating tensions in the region, TASS reported citing Bloomberg.

  • Japan-South Africa Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 05 May 2026

  • Myanmar - M. Jean-Noel Barrot's meeting with Mr Kim Aris, son of the State Counsellor of the Republic of the Union of Myanmar, Ms Aung San Suu Kyi France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 05 May 2026

  • Geography Beats Bureaucracy: Why Gwadar Is Still Key Despite Permit Problems Sputnik 05 May 2026 -- A major Chinese enterprise operating in the Gwadar Port Free Zone, has cancelled its earlier plan to exit Pakistan after receiving support from Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Planning Minister Ahsan Iqbal to resolve permit issues.

  • "Europe must not neglect democratic security amid defence efforts," says Council of Europe Secretary General in Yerevan Council of Europe 05 May 2026 -- Alain Berset joined European leaders at a time of hope in the South Caucasus
  • Remarks by President António Costa at the press conference following the EU-Armenia summit EC/CEU 05 May 2026
  • Opening remarks by President António Costa at the EU-Armenia summit EC/CEU 05 May 2026
  • EU and Armenia sign connectivity partnership, strengthen economic ties and deepen security cooperation European Commission 05 May 2026
  • Opening statement by President von der Leyen at the first EU-Armenia Summit European Commission 05 May 2026

  • United States - Jean-Noel Barrot's telephone conversation with his American counterpart Marco Rubio France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 05 May 2026
  • The Daily Press Briefing (05/05/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 05 May 2026

  • Speech by Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul at the Africa Day 2026 Germany Federal Foreign Office 05 May 2026

  • Canada's most oil-rich province nears independence vote after signature push Press TV 05 May 2026 -- A pro-independence campaign in Alberta says it has collected enough signatures to trigger a referendum on whether the province should break away from Canada.

  • China urges US to stop at once blockade and sanctions against Cuba, says FM over US' reported expansion of sanctions against Cuba Global Times 05 May 2026 -- The US' intensification of illicit and unilateral sanctions against Cuba gravely infringes on the Cuban people's right to subsistence and development and violates the basic norms in international relations. China firmly supports Cuba in safeguarding its national sovereignty and security, and opposes interference in Cuba's internal affairs, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Tuesday.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on the U.S. Ramping up Sanctions Against Cuba PRC MofA 05 May 2026

  • Türkiye's president urges EU to refrain from rhetoric that could undermine Ankara's 'constructive stance' Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 05 May 2026 -- Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan on Monday called on the EU to refrain from actions and rhetoric that could undermine Ankara's "constructive stance," adding that Brussels should praise it, Anadolu Agency reports.

  • Armenia and France sign joint declaration on establishing strategic partnership, exchange several documents Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 05 May 2026 -- The ceremony to sign the joint declaration and exchange documents was held based on the results of the state visit of President of France Emmanuel Macron to the Republic of Armenia.
  • "You have proven that this highest level of relations is reflected not only at the level of state institutions, but also of our peoples." Nikol Pashinyan and Emmanuel Macron hold an expanded meeting Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 05 May 2026 -- The official welcoming ceremony of the President of the French Republic Emmanuel Macron, who arrived in Armenia on a state visit, took place. The national anthems of Armenia and France were played during the ceremony, and then the leaders of the two countries greeted the members of the delegations. Next, the expanded meeting of the Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia Nikol Pashinyan and the President of the French Republic Emmanuel Macron took place.
  • Declaration adopted, documents signed at inaugural Armenia-EU summit Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 05 May 2026 -- Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan hosted the President of the European Council António Costa and the President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen at the presidential residence within the framework of the inaugural Armenia-EU summit.

  • Minister for Foreign Affairs presents Annual Report on International Affairs Government of Iceland 05 May 2026 -- The Minister for Foreign Affairs, Ms. Þorgerður Katrín Gunnarsdóttir, delivered her report on foreign and international affairs to Alþingi last week, highlighting key developments and special initiatives from the past year. Following her introductory remarks parliamentarians debated the report, exchanging points of view regarding the current political and global affairs outlook.

  • House to vote on impeachment articles vs. VP Sara on May 11 Philippine News Agency 05 May 2026 -- All 318 members of the House of Representatives will be given sufficient time to read the articles of impeachment against Vice President Sara Duterte before the plenary vote expected on May 11, Justice Committee chairperson Gerville Luistro of Batangas said Tuesday.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: Montenegro Edging Closer To The EU RFE/RL 05 May 2026

  • World News in Brief: Gulf tensions rise, Gaza health needs 'staggering', skills gap threat UN News 05 May 2026 -- The UN has expressed deep concern over escalating security incidents in the Gulf, warning that recent attacks risk undermining efforts to maintain regional stability.
